sessions.lever: confirmed same family plateau as func_80061658; tail-only v0/v1 rotation diff
t_for_mask: reuse single 't' pseudo for all 4 operations (arg0[0..2] + 0xFF00FF); sweep of 45 variants; exhaustive local_alloc analysis

reviewer.evidence: cheat-reviewer: t_for_mask form — pin removal + single-variable reuse falls in SOTN-sanctioned family. No register pins, no __asm__, no volatile coercion.

remaining_gap.summary: 5 instructions differ: mask in $v0 (should be $v1) — 3 register diffs + mask positioned AFTER D_800F1144 store (should be BETWEEN arg0[1] lw and sw) — 2 positional diffs. Root cause: local_alloc allocates mask pseudo to $v0 first; global_alloc then gives t $v1. Target requires inverse.
remaining_gap.allocation_analysis: Target: t(3deaths)->global_alloc->$v0, mask(1death)->local_alloc->$v1. Open-source GCC 2.7.2 port: mask gets $v0 (first available), t gets $v1 (mask's $v0 in conflict set). To get mask->$v1, a second local_alloc pseudo must occupy $v0 during mask's birth — but that pseudo also conflicts with t, pushing t out of $v0 AND $v1 (to $a0). Proven: no C structure allows simultaneous t->$v0 and mask->$v1 with this allocator.
remaining_gap.delay_slot: NOP in delay slot CONFIRMED in t_for_mask. When t uses $v0, reorg.c does not fill the jal delay slot with lw $v0 (correctly avoids clobber). This is already solved.
remaining_gap.rules_required_for_full_match: None can close the 5 remaining diffs — they are allocator-behavioral. SN Systems fork gave t->$v0 / mask->$v1; our open-source port reverses this.

next_hypotheses: Permuter (not yet tried) — may find a structurally different form that produces correct bytes via a completely different code path
Family escalation: func_80061658 (same gap, cheated with register pins) — if the family is declared needs-decision, this function follows
instrumented cc1 RTL dumps to see exact local_alloc sequencing for different C structures

rejected_forms.name: TREUSE
rejected_forms.reason: Baseline: t single pseudo (3 deaths)->$v1, mask->$v0, delay slot filled with lw $v1. Wrong allocation + wrong delay slot.

rejected_forms.name: scope_mask
rejected_forms.reason: NOP delay slot + mask->$v1 CORRECT, but t->$a0 (both $v0 and $v1 conflict). Store ordering also wrong (D_800A3464 before D_800F1144).

rejected_forms.name: t13_direct2
rejected_forms.reason: D_800F1144 = arg0[1] direct (no temp), D_800A3464 = 0xFF00FF direct, t for 1+3. Wrong allocation.

rejected_forms.name: mask_first
rejected_forms.reason: mask computed before t loads. Wrong allocation/scheduling.

rejected_forms.name: t_for_mask
rejected_forms.reason: BEST FORM (candidate). t reused for 0xFF00FF: t->$v0 CORRECT, delay slot NOP CORRECT. But mask in $v0 (should $v1) + mask positioned after D_800F1144 (should between lw/sw). 5 diffs.
